Australia and Canada

University of Sydney Scholarship for Asylum Seekers in Australia and International Students from Canada

Published 28 Jun 20264 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 28 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to University of Sydney Scholarship

The University of Sydney offers various scholarships to asylum seekers in Australia and international students from Canada, covering undergraduate and graduate degrees. These scholarships aim to support students who are facing financial difficulties in pursuing their higher education. The University of Sydney Scholarship for asylum seekers and international students provides an opportunity for students to study at one of the world's top universities.

Who Qualifies

To be eligible for the University of Sydney Scholarship, asylum seekers in Australia and international students from Canada must meet specific visa requirements. The following visa categories are eligible:

* Asylum seeker visa (subclass 866)

* Temporary Protection visa (subclass 785)

* Safe Haven Enterprise visa (subclass 790)

* Permanent Protection visa (subclass 866)

* Student visa (subclass 500) for international students from Canada

Scholarships Available

The University of Sydney offers several scholarships to support asylum seekers and international students. Here are five available scholarships:

Sydney Scholars Award

The Sydney Scholars Award provides $6,000 to $10,000 per annum for undergraduate students. To be eligible, students must have achieved an ATAR of 95 or above. The deadline for application is November 2026. For more information, visit https://www.sydney.edu.au/scholarships/sydney-scholars-award.html.

University of Sydney Access Scheme

The University of Sydney Access Scheme offers $2,000 to $5,000 per annum for undergraduate students. Eligible students must be from a low socio-economic background. The deadline for application is November 2026. For more information, visit https://www.sydney.edu.au/access-scheme.

Australia Awards Scholarship

The Australia Awards Scholarship provides full tuition fees and living costs for undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, students must be from a developing country, including Canada. The deadline for application is April 2027. For more information, visit https://www.australiaawards.gov.au.

Canada-Australia Student Mobility Program

The Canada-Australia Student Mobility Program offers $5,000 to $10,000 per annum for undergraduate and graduate students. Eligible students must be from Canada and studying at the University of Sydney. The deadline for application is November 2026. For more information, visit https://www.studyinaustralia.gov.au.

Humanitarian Scholarship

The Humanitarian Scholarship provides full tuition fees and living costs for undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, students must be asylum seekers or refugees. The deadline for application is November 2026. For more information, visit https://www.sydney.edu.au/humanitarian-scholarship.

Application Steps

To apply for the University of Sydney Scholarship, follow these steps:

  • Check your eligibility and visa requirements.

  • Choose your desired scholarship and program.

  • Prepare your documents, including academic transcripts and language proficiency test results.

  • Submit your application through the University of Sydney's online portal.

  • Pay the application fee, if required.

  • Wait for the application to be processed and reviewed.

  • Receive notification of the outcome and follow the next steps.

  • Accept the offer and enroll in the program.

Documents Checklist

To apply for the University of Sydney Scholarship, you will need to provide the following documents:

* Academic transcripts

* Language proficiency test results (e.g. IELTS or TOEFL)

* Passport and visa documents

* Proof of financial support

* Letters of recommendation

* Personal statement and research proposal (for graduate students)

Frequently Asked Questions

  • What is the eligibility criteria for the University of Sydney Scholarship?

The eligibility criteria vary depending on the scholarship, but generally, students must meet specific visa requirements, academic achievements, and language proficiency.

  • How do I apply for the University of Sydney Scholarship?

To apply, submit your application through the University of Sydney's online portal and provide the required documents.

  • What is the deadline for the University of Sydney Scholarship application?

The deadline varies depending on the scholarship, but generally, it is November 2026 or April 2027.

  • Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the University of Sydney?

Yes, you can apply for multiple scholarships, but you can only receive one award.

  • How long does it take to process the University of Sydney Scholarship application?

The processing time varies, but generally, it takes several weeks to months.

  • Can I defer my University of Sydney Scholarship?

Yes, you can defer your scholarship, but you must submit a request and provide supporting documents.

  • How do I accept my University of Sydney Scholarship offer?

To accept your offer, follow the instructions provided in your notification letter and enroll in the program.

  • What is the contact information for the University of Sydney Scholarship team?

For more information and queries, visit the University of Sydney's official website or contact the scholarship team through their online portal.

For more information on scholarships and visa requirements, visit the Australian Government Department of Home Affairs and the University of Sydney official websites.

To learn more about scholarship opportunities and application processes, visit our blog at https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.
